title: You Can't Prompt the Room: The Last Skill AI Won't Replace - Balázs Horváth, VisualLabs
summary: Balázs Horváth argues that AI code generation shifts the bottleneck upstream from coding to requirements discovery and stakeholder alignment. Using a VisualLabs hackathon where 17 of 21 agent ideas were abandoned for non-technical reasons, he contends that human skills in reading requirements, mapping processes, and naming problems precisely remain irreplaceable. He recommends story mapping, value frameworks, and VAD thinking as practical tools to validate ideas before development.
writeup: Balázs Horváth of VisualLabs argues that AI code generation moves the bottleneck upstream, from writing code to discovering requirements and aligning stakeholders. He grounds the case in a VisualLabs hackathon where 17 of 21 agent ideas were dropped for non-technical reasons, evidence that reading requirements, mapping processes, and naming problems precisely stay human work. The talk offers story mapping, value frameworks, and VAD thinking as tools to validate ideas before development begins.
